entry.title: Washington Post: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Activists in Kenya to keep fighting for abortion rights after end of Roe
  • After invasion of Ukraine, a reckoning on Russian influence in Austria

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

  • ‘Frankly it blew my mind’: how Tron changed cinema – and predicted the future of tech
  • Futura review – Italian teens discuss their lives to come in subtle and complex study
  • Anger as families of US detainees in Middle East left off Blinken call

  • Put the planet before football, UN head of Cop15 nature summit tells leaders
  • Japan deploys artificial intelligence to detect rip currents as beach season hots up
  • New Zealand faces new Covid wave as experts say moving on from pandemic is ‘wishful thinking’

  • Promised land: how South Africa’s black farmers were set up to fail

  • New Zealand Cricket strikes pay equity deal so women and men earn same match fees
  • Ukrainian flag arrives in Snake Island after Russian retreat

  • Women’s Euro 2022: our writers predict the winners and surprises
  • Model pro Nick Kyrgios shocks again with polite and efficient victory | Barney Ronay
  • Simona Halep demolishes Badosa to set up Wimbledon clash with Anisimova

entry.title: Korea Herald: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • [Photo News] President tries baby sling
  • 1st person of Korean descent wins Fields Medal

  • Gangneung welcomes tourists with portal site, ‘Visit Gangneung’

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 5 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-05 10:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Remembering Hong Kong theatre legend Ko Tin-lung, who died in his sleep aged 69. ‘He had a great eye for new talent’
  • Legco chief defends unprecedented move by new Hong Kong leader John Lee asking lawmakers to stick to specific policy areas in first question and answer session
  • Black market cigarettes worth HK$80 million seized by Hong Kong customs officers in June blitz on smuggling
  • Hong Kong’s chief executive focuses on keeping public housing waiting time from increasing – but experts says 10,000 more flats needed a year
  • Hong Kong residents’ nest eggs shrink by HK$33,300 per person as Mandatory Provident Fund posts HK$152.7 billion first-half loss
  • Hong Kong police launch hunt for three men after suspected triad member slashed, robbed of HK$300,000 and Rolex watch
  • Advisers to Hong Kong leader back 2.5 per cent pay rise for civil servants, rejecting proposed bumps as high as 7.26 per cent
  • Authorities propose Hong Kong-wide ban on feeding wild animals, with a $100,000 fine and 1 year in jail for offenders, in bid to tackle boar problem
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee vows to bring in new Covid measures, look at options to ease travellers’ inconvenience, as city logs 2,268 infections
  • Offers on Hong Kong brands, seafood, luxuries up for grabs in summer as part of tourism board initiative to boost economy
  • New Hong Kong leader John Lee says local national security law will not be rushed so all future risks can be covered
  • 1 in 5 arrests by Hong Kong’s national security police made under colonial-era sedition offence instead of 4 crimes laid out in Beijing-imposed law
  • Chinese University of Hong Kong to set up academic centre in Shanghai district, in first such collaboration with local government
  • ‘A sadness too great for words’: tributes to Ni Kuang, Hong Kong author and screenwriter, pour in from around the world
  • Gap between China, South Korea is widening as Seoul pivots to Washington
  • China pledges to share more data on the Mekong River with downstream nations
  • Fears China is planning to attack Taiwan may be overblown, leading scholar says
  • China’s new radio telescope will have dangerous solar eruptions in its gaze
  • Canadian diplomats denied access to tycoon Xiao Jianhua’s China trial
  • As US-China talks resume, an end to years of trade tariffs may not signal a soft landing in rivalry
  • China’s services activity expands at fastest in almost a year in June
  • China tiptoes towards living with Covid-19 under evolving ‘dynamic zero’ policy
  • China’s GDP target ever more distant, with second quarter growth tipped for 2-year low
  • Joe Biden may roll back China tariffs this week, US media report says
  • Foreigners are leaving China in droves. Does the nation want or need them any more?
  • US and Russian ambassadors to China clash over Ukraine war at Beijing forum
  • New Omicron strains better at dodging protection from vaccines and previous infection, study says
  • Footprints in China show dinosaurs came in from the cold to rule the world, say scientists
  • China takes aim at Nasa chief’s moon military takeover claims
  • Chinese-Canadian tycoon Xiao Jianhua, who disappeared from Hong Kong hotel, to stand trial in mainland China
  • Malaysian man flees naked on motorcycle after allegedly killing wife and baby
  • South Korea to build more nuclear power plants as phase-out plans scrapped
  • Japan hopes anti-groping Digi Police app will deter return of train molesters as crowds back after lifting of Covid-19 restrictions
  • Chinese frigate chased Russian warship away from Diaoyu Islands to stake territorial claim, Japan says
  • Singapore’s flying taxi dreams ready for take-off at its ‘other’ airport
  • Asia-Pacific leaders at Nato summit a sign of strategic shift amid Russia, China threats: analysts
  • Singapore President Halimah Yacob tests positive for Covid-19
  • Coronavirus: Thailand discourages discounts, wants high value tourists post-Covid
  • Malaysian ‘superhero’ film Mat Kilau a hit as tale of betrayal hits close to home
  • Ukraine war: Nato formally launches ratification process for Sweden, Finland membership, but Türkiye could still block vote
  • Joe Biden tries to sound upbeat on July 4, despite 309 US mass shootings this year
  • Competitive eater Joey Chestnut put a protester in a chokehold during a July 4 hot dog eating contest - and still won
  • After losing Luhansk to Russia, Ukraine forces regather for defence of Donetsk
  • Family of suspected US July 4 gunman Robert Crimo noticed nothing amiss – but social media posts paint a darker picture
  • Belt and road: can China and Russia extend their Central Asian coexistence to the South Caucasus?
  • Witnesses recount Chicago-area July 4 mass shooting in painful, chilling detail
  • Rusty Eiffel Tower needs full repairs, gets US$63 million paint job instead
  • Police arrest suspect after gunman kills 6 at US July 4 parade in Chicago suburb
  • Ukraine estimates cost of post-war reconstruction at US$750 billion
  • Ukraine war: Putin orders Russian troops to fight on after key Ukraine city taken
  • US says Al Jazeera journalist Shireen Abu Akleh likely shot by Israel but not intentionally
  • Ukraine war: blue and yellow flag of Ukraine raised again on Snake Island, military says
  • NetEase’s William Ding and Xiaomi’s Lei Jun relinquish corporate roles at video gaming units amid tightened regulation
  • Hong Kong will attract a new generation of expats, but quarantine restrictions must go first, says Link Reit’s Nicholas Allen
  • Ant Group makes privacy computing framework open source to drive adoption of its technology
  • Hong Kong government’s move to standardise land premium rates has potential to boost much-needed housing supply
  • Hong Kong stocks close higher for first time in four days as report US is set to remove China tariffs lifts recovery hopes
  • TSMC chemicals supplier warns of additional chip material price hikes, which could squeeze semiconductor giants
  • Tasty discounts: China property firms accept watermelon, garlic, peaches as partial payment for new homes amid housing slump
  • Hotels set for recovery in Asia-Pacific as borders reopen but China’s decision to remain shut clouds overall picture
  • Why US and European central banks could be source of next global financial crisis
  • Sell-off at Chinese AI giant SenseTime shows no signs of letting up as traders rush for exit to pre-empt insider selling
  • Building intelligent, sustainable enterprises in Hong Kong
  • ‘Cryptoqueen’ Ruja Ignatova added to FBI’s most wanted list, but she was already notorious in China
  • How our growing belief in sentient machines could be a problem – a Google software engineer thinks his company chatbot AI is self-aware
  • Why playing StarCraft is like running a business and how the video game changed a tech firm founder’s life
  • Hotels in Europe hire staff with no experience or résumé, as unfilled vacancies hobble hospitality sector’s rebound from the coronavirus pandemic
  • When Hong Kong’s first privately built multi-storey car park opened in 1967, with firecrackers and a fashion show to mark the occasion
  • What is one’s ‘first language’ in Hong Kong when Cantonese, English and others can meld together in the mind?
  • Composer Frank Wildhorn feels a ‘romantic connection’ with Korean audiences, and with four of his musicals playing in Seoul, the feeling appears mutual
  • The pros and cons of airbrush make-up – perfect, flawless looks without a brush or sponge as long as you’re willing to pay for it
  • Mahler, Tchaikovsky and Bruckner on Jaap van Zweden’s schedule for Hong Kong Philharmonic’s 2022-23 season
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Dior paid homage to Ukraine with an embroidery-rich autumn/winter 2022-23 collection by Maria Grazia Chiuri, riffing on Eastern European designs
  • 5 challenges Twice’s Nayeon faced before her solo debut, chart-topping ‘Im Nayeon’: a K-pop bullying scandal, an Instagram ‘ban’ … but did JYP really force her to sing all the high notes?
  • How Squid Game made HoYeon Jung a Hollywood fixture: she was in The Weeknd’s music video, bagged film roles with Lily Rose-Depp and Cate Blanchett, and models for Louis Vuitton and Chanel
  • STYLE Edit: What timepieces do movie stars wear? Zoë Kravitz, Han So-hee and Zhou Dongyu, three female ambassadors from Omega’s newest celebrity campaign, unveil their choice of women’s watches
  • Has Floyd Mayweather blown a US$1.2 billion fortune? Jake Paul claims the boxer is ‘broke’, after he splashed out on multiple Rolls-Royces, Mercedes, Lamborghinis, Porsches, mansions and private jets
  • Inside Mark Wahlberg’s blinging luxury watch collection, from the Patek Philippe Nautilus Tiffany & Co. to the Rolex Daytona Rainbow, also loved by Kevin Hart and John Mayer
  • 10 richest tennis stars of 2022 – net worths, ranked: from Rafael Nadal and Serena Williams’ Nike deals to Novak Djokovic and Andy Murray’s Adidas gigs – but which retiree earned over a billion?
  • Who is Jackie Chan’s estranged daughter, Etta Ng Chok-lam? The love child of the martial arts star and Elaine Ng, she married influencer Andi Autumn and once claimed to be ‘homeless’ in Hong Kong
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Heatstroke patients rushed to hospital triple in Tokyo

  • Canada says China bars diplomats from Canadian tycoon trial
  • Activists with Peng Shuai T-shirts searched at Wimbledon

  • Western envoys grab rare chance in China to berate Russia over Ukraine war
  • Several eastern Chinese areas in mass COVID testing to curb new waves of infections
  • Fourth crew member rescued after engineering ship sinks
  • China state museum opens in Hong Kong amid patriotism drive
  • Talks begin in Seoul to finally resolve wartime laborer dispute
  • BTS rekindle debate about military service in South Korea
  • Myanmar hosts 1st regional meeting since army takeover
  • Sydney floods burden 50,000 around Australia’s largest city
  • After losing Luhansk, Ukraine forces regather for defense of Donetsk
  • NASA satellite breaks from orbit around Earth, heads to moon
  • A turbulent U.S. this July 4, but many see cause to celebrate
  • After 3 feet of rain, 32,000 in Sydney area may need to flee
  • With craft beer and whisky, sake brewer eyes overseas markets
  • Kore-eda’s name behind body to tackle film industry woes
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/ Otsuki: Look around and you’ll find Momotaro everywhere

  • EDITORIAL: China has choked off Hong Kong’s freedom through ‘Sinicization’
  • VOX POPULI: Elections become personal if you choose your own issues to focus on
  • VOX POPULI: Workers in U.S. remind us of the importance of forming unions
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
